v.CNV: 你们的　神说：“你们要安慰，要安慰我的子民！
v.ESV: Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
v.GAE: 너희의 하나님이 이르시되 너희는 위로하라 내 백성을 위로하라
v.KJV: Comfort ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God.
v.NASB: 'Comfort, O comfort My people,' says your God.
v.NIV: Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
v.NRSV: Comfort, O comfort my people, says your God.
v.SAENEW: "너희는 위로하여라! 나의 백성을 위로하여라!" 너희의 하나님께서 말씀하신다.
v.WLB: “위로하라. 내 백성을 위로하라.” 너희의 하나님께서 말씀하신다.

v.CNV: 你们要向耶路撒冷说慈爱的话，又要向它宣告：他们的苦难已经满足了，他们的罪孽已经还清了，他们因着自己的一切罪，已经从耶和华的手里受到过重的刑罚。”
v.ESV: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and cry to her that her warfare is ended, that her iniquity is pardoned, that she has received from the Lord 's hand double for all her sins.
v.GAE: 너희는 예루살렘의 마음에 닿도록 말하며 그것에게 외치라 그 노역의 때가 끝났고 그 죄악이 사함을 받았느니라 그의 모든 죄로 말미암아 여호와의 손에서 벌을 배나 받았느니라 할지니라 하시니라
v.KJV: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the LORD’S hand double for all her sins.
v.NASB: 'Speak kindly to Jerusalem; And call out to her, that her warfare has ended, That her iniquity has been removed, That she has received of the LORD'S hand Double for all her sins.'
v.NIV: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and proclaim to her that her hard service has been completed, that her sin has been paid for, that she has received from the Lord ’s hand double for all her sins.
v.NRSV: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and cry to her that she has served her term, that her penalty is paid, that she has received from the LORD’s hand double for all her sins.
v.SAENEW: "예루살렘 주민을 격려하고, 그들에게 일러주어라. 이제 복역 기간이 끝나고, 죄에 대한 형벌도 다 받고, 지은 죄에 비하여 갑절의 벌을 주님에게서 받았다고 외쳐라."
v.WLB: “예루살렘의 마음을 위로하며 말하라. 예루살렘의 복역 기간이 완전히 끝났고 형벌도 다 치렀으며 여호와의 손에서 그 죄 값을 두 배나 받았다고 선포하라.”

v.CNV: 有声音呼喊说：“你们要在旷野清理耶和华的路，在沙漠修直我们　神的大道。
v.ESV: A voice cries: “In the wilderness prepare the way of the Lord ; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
v.GAE: 외치는 자의 소리여 이르되 너희는 광야에서 여호와의 길을 예비하라 사막에서 우리 하나님의 대로를 평탄하게 하라
v.KJV: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
v.NASB: A voice is calling, 'Clear the way for the LORD in the wilderness; Make smooth in the desert a highway for our God.
v.NIV: A voice of one calling: “In the wilderness prepare the way for the Lord ; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
v.NRSV: A voice cries out: “In the wilderness prepare the way of the LORD, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
v.SAENEW: 한 소리가 외친다. "광야에 주님께서 오실 길을 닦아라. 사막에 우리의 하나님께서 오실 큰길을 곧게 내어라.
v.WLB: 한 소리가 외친다. “광야에 여호와의 길을 내라. 사막에 우리의 하나님께서 오실 큰 길을 곧게 닦으라.

v.CNV: 一切深谷都要填满，一切山冈都要削平；险峻的要改为平地，崎岖的要改为平原。
v.ESV: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
v.GAE: 골짜기마다 돋우어지며 산마다, 언덕마다 낮아지며 고르지 아니한 곳이 평탄하게 되며 험한 곳이 평지가 될 것이요
v.KJV: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:
v.NASB: 'Let every valley be lifted up, And every mountain and hill be made low; And let the rough ground become a plain, And the rugged terrain a broad valley;
v.NIV: Every valley shall be raised up, every mountain and hill made low; the rough ground shall become level, the rugged places a plain.
v.NRSV: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
v.SAENEW: 모든 계곡은 메우고, 산과 언덕은 깎아 내리고, 거친 길은 평탄하게 하고, 험한 곳은 평지로 만들어라.
v.WLB: 모든 골짜기는 높이고 모든 산과 언덕은 낮추고 가파른 곳은 고르게 하고 울퉁불퉁한 곳은 평지로 만들라.

v.CNV: 耶和华的荣耀必要显现，所有的人都必一同看见，因为这是耶和华亲口说的。”
v.ESV: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.”
v.GAE: 여호와의 영광이 나타나고 모든 육체가 그것을 함께 보리라 이는 여호와의 입이 말씀하셨느니라
v.KJV: And the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the LORD hath spoken it.
v.NASB: Then the glory of the LORD will be revealed, And all flesh will see it together; For the mouth of the LORD has spoken.'
v.NIV: And the glory of the Lord will be revealed, and all people will see it together. For the mouth of the Lord has spoken.”
v.NRSV: Then the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, and all people shall see it together, for the mouth of the LORD has spoken.”
v.SAENEW: 주님의 영광이 나타날 것이니, 모든 사람이 그것을 함께 볼 것이다. 이것은 주님께서 친히 약속하신 것이다."
v.WLB: 여호와의 영광이 드러날 것이니 모든 사람이 그것을 함께 보게 될 것이다. 여호와께서 친히 입으로 말씀하셨다.”

v.CNV: 有声音说：“你呼喊吧！”他问：“我呼喊什么呢？”“所有的人尽都如草；他们的荣美都像野地的花。
v.ESV: A voice says, “Cry!” And I said, “What shall I cry?” All flesh is grass, and all its beauty is like the flower of the field.
v.GAE: 말하는 자의 소리여 이르되 외치라 대답하되 내가 무엇이라 외치리이까 하니 이르되 모든 육체는 풀이요 그의 모든 아름다움은 들의 꽃과 같으니
v.KJV: The voice said, Cry. And he said, What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof is as the flower of the field:
v.NASB: A voice says, 'Call out.' Then he answered, 'What shall I call out?' All flesh is grass, and all its loveliness is like the flower of the field.
v.NIV: A voice says, “Cry out.” And I said, “What shall I cry?” “All people are like grass, and all their faithfulness is like the flowers of the field.
v.NRSV: A voice says, “Cry out!” And I said, “What shall I cry?” All people are grass, their constancy is like the flower of the field.
v.SAENEW: 한 소리가 외친다. "너는 외쳐라." 그래서 내가 "무엇이라고 외쳐야 합니까?" 하고 물었다. "모든 육체는 풀이요, 그의 모든 아름다움은 들의 꽃과 같을 뿐이다.
v.WLB: 한 소리가 명령한다. “외치라.” 그래서 내가 말했다. “무엇이라고 외쳐야 합니까?” “모든 사람은 풀과 같고 그 모든 아름다움은 들판의 꽃과 같다.

v.CNV: 草必枯干，花必凋谢；因为耶和华的气吹在上面；真的，这民的确是草！
v.ESV: The grass withers, the flower fades when the breath of the Lord blows on it; surely the people are grass.
v.GAE: 풀은 마르고 꽃이 시듦은 여호와의 기운이 그 위에 붊이라 이 백성은 실로 풀이로다
v.KJV: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the LORD bloweth upon it: surely the people is grass.
v.NASB: The grass withers, the flower fades, When the breath of the LORD blows upon it; Surely the people are grass.
v.NIV: The grass withers and the flowers fall, because the breath of the Lord blows on them. Surely the people are grass.
v.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades, when the breath of the LORD blows upon it; surely the people are grass.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 그 위에 입김을 부시면, 풀은 마르고 꽃은 시든다. 그렇다. 이 백성은 풀에 지나지 않는다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서 입김을 부시면 풀은 시들고 꽃은 떨어진다. 그렇다. 이 백성은 풀에 불과하다.

v.CNV: 草必枯干，花必凋谢，唯有我们　神的道永远长存。”
v.ESV: The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God will stand forever.
v.GAE: 풀은 마르고 꽃은 시드나 우리 하나님의 말씀은 영원히 서리라 하라
v.KJV: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
v.NASB: The grass withers, the flower fades, But the word of our God stands forever.
v.NIV: The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the word of our God endures forever. ”
v.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades; but the word of our God will stand forever.
v.SAENEW: 풀은 마르고 꽃은 시드나, 우리 하나님의 말씀은 영원히 서 있다."
v.WLB: 풀은 시들고 꽃은 떨어지지만 우리 하나님의 말씀은 영원히 서 있다.”

v.CNV: 报好信息给锡安的啊！你要登上高山。报好信息给耶路撒冷的啊！你要极力扬声。要扬声，不要惧怕！要对犹大的众城说：“看哪，你们的　神！”
v.ESV: Go on up to a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good news; lift up your voice with strength, O Jerusalem, herald of good news; lift it up, fear not; say to the cities of Judah, “Behold your God!”
v.GAE: 아름다운 소식을 시온에 전하는 자여 너는 높은 산에 오르라 아름다운 소식을 예루살렘에 전하는 자여 너는 힘써 소리를 높이라 두려워하지 말고 소리를 높여 유다의 성읍들에게 이르기를 너희의 하나님을 보라 하라
v.KJV: O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!
v.NASB: Get yourself up on a high mountain, O Zion, bearer of good news, Lift up your voice mightily, O Jerusalem, bearer of good news; Lift it up, do not fear. Say to the cities of Judah, 'Here is your God!'
v.NIV: You who bring good news to Zion, go up on a high mountain. You who bring good news to Jerusalem, lift up your voice with a shout, lift it up, do not be afraid; say to the towns of Judah, “Here is your God!”
v.NRSV: Get you up to a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good tidings; lift up your voice with strength, O Jerusalem, herald of good tidings, lift it up, do not fear; say to the cities of Judah, “Here is your God!”
v.SAENEW: 좋은 소식을 전하는 시온아, 어서 높은 산으로 올라가거라. 아름다운 소식을 전하는 예루살렘아, 너의 목소리를 힘껏 높여라. 두려워하지 말고 소리를 높여라. 유다의 성읍들에게 "여기에 너희의 하나님이 계신다" 하고 말하여라.
v.WLB: 시온에 좋은 소식을 전하는 사람아, 너는 높은 산으로 올라가거라. 예루살렘에 좋은 소식을 전하는 사람아, 두려워하지 말고 힘껏 외쳐라. 유다의 성읍들에게 말하여라. “보라. 너희 하나님께서 여기 계시다!”

v.CNV: 看哪！主耶和华必像大能者临到，他的膀臂要为他掌权；看哪！他给予人的赏赐在他那里，他施予人的报应在他面前。
v.ESV: Behold, the Lord God comes with might, and his arm rules for him; behold, his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.
v.GAE: 보라 주 여호와께서 장차 강한 자로 임하실 것이요 친히 그의 팔로 다스리실 것이라 보라 상급이 그에게 있고 보응이 그의 앞에 있으며
v.KJV: Behold, the Lord GOD will come with strong hand, and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward is with him, and his work before him.
v.NASB: Behold, the Lord GOD will come with might, With His arm ruling for Him. Behold, His reward is with Him And His recompense before Him.
v.NIV: See, the Sovereign Lord comes with power, and he rules with a mighty arm. See, his reward is with him, and his recompense accompanies him.
v.NRSV: See, the Lord GOD comes with might, and his arm rules for him; his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.
v.SAENEW: 만군의 주 하나님께서 오신다. 그가 권세를 잡고 친히 다스리실 것이다. 보아라, 그가 백성에게 주실 상급을 가지고 오신다. 백성에게 주실 보상을 가지고 오신다.
v.WLB: 보라. 주 여호와께서 능력으로 오신다. 친히 그 팔로 다스리실 것이다. 보라. 그분이 상급을 갖고 오신다. 그분의 보상이 그분의 앞에 있다.

v.CNV: 他必像牧人牧养自己的羊群，像用膀臂聚集羊羔，抱在自己的怀中，慢慢引导乳养小羊的。
v.ESV: He will tend his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms; he will carry them in his bosom, and gently lead those that are with young.
v.GAE: 그는 목자 같이 양 떼를 먹이시며 어린 양을 그 팔로 모아 품에 안으시며 젖먹이는 암컷들을 온순히 인도하시리로다
v.KJV: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.
v.NASB: Like a shepherd He will tend His flock, In His arm He will gather the lambs And carry them in His bosom; He will gently lead the nursing ewes.
v.NIV: He tends his flock like a shepherd: He gathers the lambs in his arms and carries them close to his heart; he gently leads those that have young.
v.NRSV: He will feed his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms, and carry them in his bosom, and gently lead the mother sheep.
v.SAENEW: 그는 목자와 같이 그의 양 떼를 먹이시며, 어린 양들을 팔로 모으시고, 품에 안으시며, 젖을 먹이는 어미 양들을 조심스럽게 이끄신다.
v.WLB: 그분은 목자처럼 자신의 양 떼를 돌보시고 자신의 어린 양들을 양 팔로 끌어안아 가슴에 품으시고 젖먹이 딸린 양들을 고이고이 이끄신다.

v.CNV: 谁曾用掌心量过海水，用手掌测过苍天呢？谁曾用升斗量过大地的尘土，用秤称山岭，用天平称冈陵呢？
v.ESV: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens with a span, enclosed the dust of the earth in a measure and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
v.GAE: 누가 손바닥으로 바닷물을 헤아렸으며 뼘으로 하늘을 쟀으며 땅의 티끌을 되에 담아 보았으며 접시 저울로 산들을, 막대 저울로 언덕들을 달아 보았으랴
v.KJV: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
v.NASB: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of His hand, And marked off the heavens by the span, And calculated the dust of the earth by the measure, And weighed the mountains in a balance And the hills in a pair of scales?
v.NIV: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, or with the breadth of his hand marked off the heavens? Who has held the dust of the earth in a basket, or weighed the mountains on the scales and the hills in a balance?
v.NRSV: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens with a span, enclosed the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
v.SAENEW: 누가 바닷물을 손바닥으로 떠서 헤아려 보았으며, 뼘으로 하늘을 재어 보았느냐? 누가 온 땅의 티끌을 되로 되어 보고, 산들을 어깨 저울로 달아 보고, 언덕들을 손저울로 달아 보았느냐?
v.WLB: 누가 손바닥으로 바닷물을 헤아렸고 뼘으로 하늘을 쟀느냐? 누가 땅의 흙을 바구니에 담았고 저울로 산을 달았으며 천칭으로 언덕을 달았느냐?

v.CNV: 谁曾测度耶和华的灵，或作过他的谋士教导他呢？
v.ESV: Who has measured the Spirit of the Lord , or what man shows him his counsel?
v.GAE: 누가 여호와의 영을 지도하였으며 그의 모사가 되어 그를 가르쳤으랴
v.KJV: Who hath directed the Spirit of the LORD, or being his counsellor hath taught him?
v.NASB: Who has directed the Spirit of the LORD, Or as His counselor has informed Him?
v.NIV: Who can fathom the Spirit of the Lord , or instruct the Lord as his counselor?
v.NRSV: Who has directed the spirit of the LORD, or as his counselor has instructed him?
v.SAENEW: 누가 주님의 영을 헤아릴 수 있겠으며, 주님의 조언자가 되어 그를 가르칠 수 있겠느냐?
v.WLB: 누가 여호와의 마음을 헤아렸고 그분의 상담자가 돼 그분을 가르쳤느냐?

v.CNV: 他与谁商议，谁使他有聪明？谁把正确的路指教他，把知识指教他，又把明智的路教导他呢？
v.ESV: Whom did he consult, and who made him understand? Who taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of understanding?
v.GAE: 그가 누구와 더불어 의논하셨으며 누가 그를 교훈하였으며 그에게 정의의 길로 가르쳤으며 지식을 가르쳤으며 통달의 도를 보여 주었느냐
v.KJV: With whom took he counsel, and who instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?
v.NASB: With whom did He consult and who gave Him understanding? And who taught Him in the path of justice and taught Him knowledge And informed Him of the way of understanding?
v.NIV: Whom did the Lord consult to enlighten him, and who taught him the right way? Who was it that taught him knowledge, or showed him the path of understanding?
v.NRSV: Whom did he consult for his enlightenment, and who taught him the path of justice? Who taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of understanding?
v.SAENEW: 그가 누구와 의논하시는가? 누가 그를 깨우쳐 드리며, 공평의 도리를 가르쳐 드리는가? 누가 그에게 지식을 가르쳐 드리며, 슬기로운 처세술을 가르쳐 드리는가?
v.WLB: 누구에게 여호와께서 자신을 이해시켜 달라고 요청했느냐? 누가 그분에게 공의의 길을 가르쳤고 지식을 가르쳤으며 분별의 길을 알려드렸느냐?

v.CNV: 看哪！万国都像水桶里的一滴，又被看作天平上的微尘。看哪！他举起众海岛，好像微细之物。
v.ESV: Behold, the nations are like a drop from a bucket, and are accounted as the dust on the scales; behold, he takes up the coastlands like fine dust.
v.GAE: 보라 그에게는 열방이 통의 한 방울 물과 같고 저울의 작은 티끌 같으며 섬들은 떠오르는 먼지 같으리니
v.KJV: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.
v.NASB: Behold, the nations are like a drop from a bucket, And are regarded as a speck of dust on the scales; Behold, He lifts up the islands like fine dust.
v.NIV: Surely the nations are like a drop in a bucket; they are regarded as dust on the scales; he weighs the islands as though they were fine dust.
v.NRSV: Even the nations are like a drop from a bucket, and are accounted as dust on the scales; see, he takes up the isles like fine dust.
v.SAENEW: 그에게는 뭇 나라가, 고작해야, 두레박에서 떨어지는 한 방울 물이나, 저울 위의 티끌과 같을 뿐이다. 섬들도 먼지를 들어 올리듯 가볍게 들어 올리신다.
v.WLB: 보라. 뭇 민족들은 물통에 담긴 물 한 방울 같고 저울 위에 놓인 흙과 같을 뿐이다. 섬들도 고운 흙보다 더 가볍다.

v.CNV: 黎巴嫩的树林不够当柴烧，其中的走兽也不够作燔祭。
v.ESV: Lebanon would not suffice for fuel, nor are its beasts enough for a burnt offering.
v.GAE: 레바논은 땔감에도 부족하겠고 그 짐승들은 번제에도 부족할 것이라
v.KJV: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
v.NASB: Even Lebanon is not enough to burn, Nor its beasts enough for a burnt offering.
v.NIV: Lebanon is not sufficient for altar fires, nor its animals enough for burnt offerings.
v.NRSV: Lebanon would not provide fuel enough, nor are its animals enough for a burnt offering.
v.SAENEW: 레바논의 삼림이 제단의 장작으로 충분하지 않고, 그 곳의 짐승들도 번제물로 드리기에 충분하지 않다.
v.WLB: 레바논의 숲도 제단의 장작으로 쓰기에 모자라고 그곳의 짐승들도 번제물로는 충분하지 않다.

v.CNV: 万国在他跟前好像不存在，在他看来，只是乌有和虚空。
v.ESV: All the nations are as nothing before him, they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
v.GAE: 그의 앞에는 모든 열방이 아무것도 아니라 그는 그들을 없는 것 같이, 빈 것 같이 여기시느니라
v.KJV: All nations before him are as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.
v.NASB: All the nations are as nothing before Him, They are regarded by Him as less than nothing and meaningless.
v.NIV: Before him all the nations are as nothing; they are regarded by him as worthless and less than nothing.
v.NRSV: All the nations are as nothing before him; they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
v.SAENEW: 그 앞에서는 모든 민족이 아무것도 아니며, 그에게는 사람이란 전혀 없는 것이나 다름이 없다.
v.WLB: 그분 앞에서는 모든 민족이 아무것도 아니다. 그분은 그들을 아무것도 아닌 것보다 더 하찮게 여기신다.

v.CNV: 你们把谁来跟　神相比呢？你们用什么形象来与　神并列呢？
v.ESV: To whom then will you liken God, or what likeness compare with him?
v.GAE: 그런즉 너희가 하나님을 누구와 같다 하겠으며 무슨 형상을 그에게 비기겠느냐
v.KJV: To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
v.NASB: To whom then will you liken God? Or what likeness will you compare with Him?
v.NIV: With whom, then, will you compare God? To what image will you liken him?
v.NRSV: To whom then will you liken God, or what likeness compare with him?
v.SAENEW: 그렇다면, 너희가 하나님을 누구와 같다 하겠으며, 어떤 형상에 비기겠느냐?
v.WLB: 그렇다면 하나님을 누구와 견주겠으며 어떤 형상과 닮았다고 하겠는가?

v.CNV: 至于偶像，是匠人铸造的，铸匠用金子把它包裹，用银子为它做银链。
v.ESV: An idol! A craftsman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold and casts for it silver chains.
v.GAE: 우상은 장인이 부어 만들었고 장색이 금으로 입혔고 또 은 사슬을 만든 것이니라
v.KJV: The workman melteth a graven image, and the goldsmith spreadeth it over with gold, and casteth silver chains.
v.NASB: As for the idol, a craftsman casts it, A goldsmith plates it with gold, And a silversmith fashions chains of silver.
v.NIV: As for an idol, a metalworker casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold and fashions silver chains for it.
v.NRSV: An idol? — A workman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold, and casts for it silver chains.
v.SAENEW: 우상이란 대장장이가 부어 만들고, 도금장이가 금으로 입히고, 은사슬을 만들어 걸친 것이다.
v.WLB: 우상? 그것은 장인들이 부어 만들고 도금장이가 그 위에 금을 입힌 다음 은으로 장식한 물건일 뿐이다.

v.CNV: 贫穷献不起这供物的，就拣选不朽坏的树木，为自己寻找巧匠，立起不会动摇的偶像。
v.ESV: He who is too impoverished for an offering chooses wood that will not rot; he seeks out a skillful craftsman to set up an idol that will not move.
v.GAE: 궁핍한 자는 거제를 드릴 때에 썩지 아니하는 나무를 택하고 지혜로운 장인을 구하여 우상을 만들어 흔들리지 아니하도록 세우느니라
v.KJV: He that is so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree that will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, that shall not be moved.
v.NASB: He who is too impoverished for such an offering Selects a tree that does not rot; He seeks out for himself a skillful craftsman To prepare an idol that will not totter.
v.NIV: A person too poor to present such an offering selects wood that will not rot; they look for a skilled worker to set up an idol that will not topple.
v.NRSV: As a gift one chooses mulberry wood — wood that will not rot — then seeks out a skilled artisan to set up an image that will not topple.
v.SAENEW: 금이나 은을 구할 형편이 못되는 사람은 썩지 않는 나무를 골라서 구하여 놓고, 넘어지지 않을 우상을 만들려고 숙련된 기술자를 찾는다.
v.WLB: 너무 가난해서 그런 물건을 구할 수 없는 사람은 썩지 않는 나무를 골라서 숙련된 기술자를 찾아가 썩지 않을 우상을 만든다.

v.CNV: 你们不曾知道吗？你们不曾听见吗？不是从起初就已经告诉过你们吗？自从大地的根基立了以来，你们还不曾明白吗？
v.ESV: Do you not know? Do you not hear? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
v.GAE: 너희가 알지 못하였느냐 너희가 듣지 못하였느냐 태초부터 너희에게 전하지 아니하였느냐 땅의 기초가 창조될 때부터 너희가 깨닫지 못하였느냐
v.KJV: Have ye not known? have ye not heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have ye not understood from the foundations of the earth?
v.NASB: Do you not know? Have you not heard? Has it not been declared to you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
v.NIV: Do you not know? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood since the earth was founded?
v.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
v.SAENEW: 너희가 알지 못하였느냐? 너희가 듣지 못하였느냐? 태초부터 너희가 전해 들은 것이 아니냐? 너희는 땅의 기초가 어떻게 세워졌는지 알지 못하였느냐?
v.WLB: 너희는 알지 못하느냐? 너희는 듣지 못했느냐? 태초부터 그것을 듣지 않았느냐? 땅의 기초가 세워지기 전부터 너희가 잘 알고 있지 않느냐?

v.CNV: 神坐在大地的圆穹之上，地上的居民好像蚱蜢，他铺张诸天如铺张幔子，展开众天像展开可以居住的帐棚。
v.ESV: It is he who sits above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to dwell in;
v.GAE: 그는 땅 위 궁창에 앉으시나니 땅에 사는 사람들은 메뚜기 같으니라 그가 하늘을 차일 같이 펴셨으며 거주할 천막 같이 치셨고
v.KJV: It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as grasshoppers; that stretcheth out the heavens as a curtain, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in:
v.NASB: It is He who sits above the circle of the earth, And its inhabitants are like grasshoppers, Who stretches out the heavens like a curtain And spreads them out like a tent to dwell in.
v.NIV: He sits enthroned above the circle of the earth, and its people are like grasshoppers. He stretches out the heavens like a canopy, and spreads them out like a tent to live in.
v.NRSV: It is he who sits above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to live in;
v.SAENEW: 땅 위의 저 푸른 하늘에 계신 분께서 세상을 만드셨다. 땅에 사는 사람들은 하나님 보시기에는 메뚜기와 같을 뿐이다. 그는 하늘을, 마치 엷은 휘장처럼 펴셔서, 사람이 사는 장막처럼 쳐 놓으셨다.
v.WLB: 그는 땅 위의 둥근 천장에 앉으시니 땅의 백성들은 메뚜기와 같다. 그분은 하늘을 휘장처럼 펴시고 사람이 사는 장막처럼 그것을 펼치신다.

v.CNV: 他使诸侯都归于无有，使地上的审判官成为虚空。
v.ESV: who brings princes to nothing, and makes the rulers of the earth as emptiness.
v.GAE: 귀인들을 폐하시며 세상의 사사들을 헛되게 하시나니
v.KJV: That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
v.NASB: He it is who reduces rulers to nothing, Who makes the judges of the earth meaningless.
v.NIV: He brings princes to naught and reduces the rulers of this world to nothing.
v.NRSV: who brings princes to naught, and makes the rulers of the earth as nothing.
v.SAENEW: 그는 통치자들을 허수아비로 만드시며, 땅의 지배자들을 쓸모 없는 사람으로 만드신다.
v.WLB: 그분은 귀족들을 있으나마나한 사람으로 만들고 세상 통치자들을 쓸모없는 사람으로 만드신다.

v.CNV: 他们才刚刚栽上，刚刚种上，他们的树头刚刚在地里扎根，他一吹在其上，他们就枯干了，旋风把他们像碎秸一样吹去。
v.ESV: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows on them, and they wither, and the tempest carries them off like stubble.
v.GAE: 그들은 겨우 심기고 겨우 뿌려졌으며 그 줄기가 겨우 땅에 뿌리를 박자 곧 하나님이 입김을 부시니 그들은 말라 회오리바람에 불려 가는 초개 같도다
v.KJV: Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.
v.NASB: Scarcely have they been planted, Scarcely have they been sown, Scarcely has their stock taken root in the earth, But He merely blows on them, and they wither, And the storm carries them away like stubble.
v.NIV: No sooner are they planted, no sooner are they sown, no sooner do they take root in the ground, than he blows on them and they wither, and a whirlwind sweeps them away like chaff.
v.NRSV: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows upon them, and they wither, and the tempest carries them off like stubble.
v.SAENEW: 이 세상의 통치자들은 풀포기와 같다. 심기가 무섭게, 씨를 뿌리기가 무섭게, 뿌리를 내리기가 무섭게, 하나님께서 입김을 부셔서 말려 버리시니, 마치 강풍에 날리는 검불과 같다.
v.WLB: 그들이 심어지자마자, 씨가 뿌려지자마자, 땅에 뿌리를 내리자마자, 그분은 입김을 불어 그들을 말려 버리시고 회오리바람이 그들을 겨처럼 쓸고 지나가게 하실 것이다.

v.CNV: 那圣者说：“你们把谁来跟我相比，使他与我相等呢？”
v.ESV: To whom then will you compare me, that I should be like him? says the Holy One.
v.GAE: 거룩하신 이가 이르시되 그런즉 너희가 나를 누구에게 비교하여 나를 그와 동등하게 하겠느냐 하시니라
v.KJV: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal? saith the Holy One.
v.NASB: 'To whom then will you liken Me That I would be his equal?' says the Holy One.
v.NIV: “To whom will you compare me? Or who is my equal?” says the Holy One.
v.NRSV: To whom then will you compare me, or who is my equal? says the Holy One.
v.SAENEW: 거룩하신 분께서 말씀하신다. "그렇다면, 너희가 나를 누구와 견주겠으며, 나를 누구와 같다고 하겠느냐?"
v.WLB: “너희는 나를 누구와 견주겠느냐? 누가 나와 같겠느냐?” 거룩하신 분이 말씀하신다.

v.CNV: 你们向天举目吧！看是谁创造了这些万象？是谁按着数目把万象领出来，一一指名呼唤？因他的大能大力，连一个也不缺少。
v.ESV: Lift up your eyes on high and see: who created these? He who brings out their host by number, calling them all by name; by the greatness of his might and because he is strong in power, not one is missing.
v.GAE: 너희는 눈을 높이 들어 누가 이 모든 것을 창조하였나 보라 주께서는 수효대로 만상을 이끌어 내시고 그들의 모든 이름을 부르시나니 그의 권세가 크고 그의 능력이 강하므로 하나도 빠짐이 없느니라
v.KJV: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.
v.NASB: Lift up your eyes on high And see who has created these stars, The One who leads forth their host by number, He calls them all by name; Because of the greatness of His might and the strength of His power, Not one of them is missing.
v.NIV: Lift up your eyes and look to the heavens: Who created all these? He who brings out the starry host one by one and calls forth each of them by name. Because of his great power and mighty strength, not one of them is missing.
v.NRSV: Lift up your eyes on high and see: Who created these? He who brings out their host and numbers them, calling them all by name; because he is great in strength, mighty in power, not one is missing.
v.SAENEW: 너희는 고개를 들어서, 저 위를 바라보아라. 누가 이 모든 별을 창조하였느냐? 바로 그분께서 천체를 수효를 세어 불러내신다. 그는 능력이 많으시고 힘이 세셔서, 하나하나, 이름을 불러 나오게 하시니, 하나도 빠지는 일이 없다.
v.WLB: 눈을 높이 들어 위를 쳐다 보라. 누가 이 모든 것을 창조했느냐? 그분은 별자리들을 하나하나 불러내어 그것들을 각각 이름대로 부르신다. 그분의 능력은 크시고 힘은 강하시니 하나도 빠지지 않고 부르신다.

v.CNV: 雅各啊！你为什么这样说；以色列啊！你为什么埋怨说：“我的道路向耶和华隐藏，我的案件被我的　神忽略了”？
v.ESV: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the Lord , and my right is disregarded by my God”?
v.GAE: 야곱아 어찌하여 네가 말하며 이스라엘아 네가 이르기를 내 길은 여호와께 숨겨졌으며 내 송사는 내 하나님에게서 벗어난다 하느냐
v.KJV: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the LORD, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
v.NASB: Why do you say, O Jacob, and assert, O Israel, 'My way is hidden from the LORD, And the justice due me escapes the notice of my God '?
v.NIV: Why do you complain, Jacob? Why do you say, Israel, “My way is hidden from the Lord ; my cause is disregarded by my God”?
v.NRSV: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the LORD, and my right is disregarded by my God”?
v.SAENEW: 야곱아, 네가 어찌하여 불평하며, 이스라엘아, 네가 어찌하여 불만을 토로하느냐? 어찌하여 "주님께서는 나의 사정을 모르시고, 하나님께서는 나의 정당한 권리를 지켜 주시지 않는다" 하느냐?
v.WLB: 야곱아, 네가 왜 이렇게 말하느냐? 이스라엘아, 네가 왜 이렇게 이야기하느냐? 왜 “내 길은 여호와께 숨겨져 있고 내 공의는 하나님이 무시하신다” 하느냐?

v.CNV: 你不知道吗？你没有听过吗？永在的　神、耶和华、地极的创造主既不疲乏，也不困倦；他的知识无法测度。
v.ESV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is unsearchable.
v.GAE: 너는 알지 못하였느냐 듣지 못하였느냐 영원하신 하나님 여호와, 땅 끝까지 창조하신 이는 피곤하지 않으시며 곤비하지 않으시며 명철이 한이 없으시며
v.KJV: Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.
v.NASB: Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Everlasting God, the LORD, the Creator of the ends of the earth Does not become weary or tired. His understanding is inscrutable.
v.NIV: Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary, and his understanding no one can fathom.
v.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is unsearchable.
v.SAENEW: 너는 알지 못하였느냐? 너는 듣지 못하였느냐? 주님은 영원하신 하나님이시다. 땅 끝까지 창조하신 분이시다. 그는 피곤을 느끼지 않으시며, 지칠 줄을 모르시며, 그 지혜가 무궁하신 분이시다.
v.WLB: 너희가 알지 못하느냐? 너희가 듣지 못했느냐? 여호와는 영원한 하나님이시고 땅 끝을 창조한 분이시다. 그분은 지치거나 피곤해하지 않으시고 그분의 통찰력은 아무도 탐구할 수 없다.

v.CNV: 疲乏的，他赐气力，无力的，他加力量。
v.ESV: He gives power to the faint, and to him who has no might he increases strength.
v.GAE: 피곤한 자에게는 능력을 주시며 무능한 자에게는 힘을 더하시나니
v.KJV: He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
v.NASB: He gives strength to the weary, And to him who lacks might He increases power.
v.NIV: He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak.
v.NRSV: He gives power to the faint, and strengthens the powerless.
v.SAENEW: 피곤한 사람에게 힘을 주시며, 기운을 잃은 사람에게 기력을 주시는 분이시다.
v.WLB: 그분은 지친 사람들에게 힘을 주시고 약한 사람들에게 힘을 북돋워 주신다.

v.CNV: 就是年轻人也会疲乏困倦，强壮的人也会全然跌倒。
v.ESV: Even youths shall faint and be weary, and young men shall fall exhausted;
v.GAE: 소년이라도 피곤하며 곤비하며 장정이라도 넘어지며 쓰러지되
v.KJV: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall:
v.NASB: Though youths grow weary and tired, And vigorous young men stumble badly,
v.NIV: Even youths grow tired and weary, and young men stumble and fall;
v.NRSV: Even youths will faint and be weary, and the young will fall exhausted;
v.SAENEW: 비록 젊은이들이 피곤하여 지치고, 장정들이 맥없이 비틀거려도,
v.WLB: 젊은이라도 지쳐 피곤하고 장정이라도 걸려 비틀거리겠지만

v.CNV: 但那些仰望耶和华的人，必重新得力；他们必像鹰一样展翅上腾；他们奔跑，也不困倦，他们行走，也不疲乏。
v.ESV: but they who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles; they shall run and not be weary; they shall walk and not faint.
v.GAE: 오직 여호와를 앙망하는 자는 새 힘을 얻으리니 독수리가 날개치며 올라감 같을 것이요 달음박질하여도 곤비하지 아니하겠고 걸어가도 피곤하지 아니하리로다
v.KJV: But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
v.NASB: Yet those who wait for the LORD Will gain new strength; They will mount up with wings like eagles, They will run and not get tired, They will walk and not become weary.
v.NIV: but those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not be faint.
v.NRSV: but those who wait for the LORD shall renew their strength, they shall mount up with wings like eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.
v.SAENEW: 오직 주님을 소망으로 삼는 사람은 새 힘을 얻으리니, 독수리가 날개를 치며 솟아오르듯 올라갈 것이요, 뛰어도 지치지 않으며, 걸어도 피곤하지 않을 것이다.
v.WLB: 여호와를 바라는 사람들은 새로운 힘을 얻을 것이다. 독수리가 날개를 치면서 솟구치듯 올라갈 것이고 아무리 달려도 지치지 않고 아무리 걸어도 피곤하지 않을 것이다.
